attributes.description: Alerts when a RabbitMQ node raises the disk free alarm (`rabbitmq.node.disk_free_alarm > 0`). Publishing is halted on the affected node.
attributes.name: [RabbitMQ OTel] Disk alarm triggered

investigation_guide.blob:
## RabbitMQ disk alarm triggered

### What fired
`rabbitmq.node.disk_free_alarm > 0` on at least one node.

### Why it matters
When a node's free disk drops below the configured threshold, RabbitMQ halts all publishing on that node to protect against running out of space entirely.

### Triage
1. Identify the affected node and check disk usage.
2. Inspect queue depths and retention.

### Remediation
- Free disk space (archive / delete old data).
- Grow the volume.

### Tuning
- Always P1.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-rabbitmqreceiver.otel-default
// Disk alarm: when triggered (1), publishing is halted
// Requires node-level metrics enabled in receiver config
| WHERE metrics.rabbitmq.node.disk_free_alarm IS NOT NULL
  AND metrics.rabbitmq.node.disk_free_alarm > 0
| STATS alarm_count = COUNT(*)
  BY resource.attributes.rabbitmq.node.name
| WHERE alarm_count > 0
